free_text: Nuclear structure study of semi-magic 125Sn via
(n,gamma) and (d,p) reactions
Private communication
LVR-15 reactor
The target of about 3.0 g with the enrichment of
97.9% in 124Sn was inserted into a 6Li-target holder.
This holder was placed between the detectors. In this
position, the three metallic target pellets each of
10 mm diameter were irradiated with a thermal neutron
beam.
Thermal neutron beam of about 10**6 n/cm2/s from the
external mirror neutron guide.
Two HPGe detectors with relative efficiencies
of 25% and 28% and with the energy resolution of 1.9
keV at 1332 keV and 4.8 keV at 6 MeV for the both
detectors.
Results
from (d,p) reaction are included in Entry D0457
Entry approved by I.Tomandl
SD
VS. REFERENCE, STATUS in subentry 2 updated
From R.B.Firestone,S.Y.F.Chu,C.M. Baglin(Eds.),
Table of Isotopes, Wiley 1999.
Decay data.
The systematic uncertainty in absolute
intensities taking into account the population ratio
of the ground state and the isomeric state and the
absolute intensity of this line per 100 decays, and
assuming an equilibrium during irradiation of the
124Sn target we converted the relative intensities
of gamma-rays into absolute intensities, the number
of emitted gamma-rays per 100 captured thermal neutrons
Statistical error
Only statistical errors are given for the gamma
intensities. A systematic error of 5% has to be added
in quadrature to relative intensity errors.
Data from the table 5 in J,PR/C,83,044326,2011
